question_text: Explain why different foods provide different amounts of energy.
correct_answer: Different foods contain different types and amounts of nutrients (carbohydrates, proteins, and fats) and different proportions of water and other materials, which affect their total energy content.
explanation: Foods like oil and nuts are high in fat and provide lots of energy, while water-rich foods like lettuce have very little energy. The type and amount of nutrients in each food determines how much chemical energy it stores.
